message:
feat(harness): decoding pipeline verification tests (bd-1hi.4)

22 tests covering the 6-step RaptorQ decode pipeline: peeling,
Gaussian elimination, erasure recovery, DecodeProof artifacts,
performance gate (K=64 T=4096 <10ms), statistical success rates,
mixed ISI decode, and E2E roundtrips for K in {5..500}.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -97,7 +97,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1s71","title":"§5.6.5 GC Coordination + In-Process Version Pruning","description":"STATUS: CLOSED (superseded rollup)

This bead is a consolidated/rolled-up version of §5.6.5 created during an earlier spec-to-beads conversion pass.

DO NOT implement from this rollup directly.

Use these replacement beads instead (plan-of-record):
- bd-zcdn — §5.6.5 GC Coordination: Horizon, Scheduling, Incremental Pruning
- bd-3t3.10 — §5.6.5.1 In-Process Version Pruning (Incremental, Touched-Page-Driven)

---

SECTION: §5.6.5 + §5.6.5.1 (spec lines ~8012-8147)

PURPOSE: Implement the gc_horizon computation and incremental version chain pruning.

## gc_horizon (SharedMemoryLayout)
- gc_horizon is a monotonically increasing CommitSeq safe-point: min(begin_seq) across all active txns
- Since begin_seq derives from monotonically increasing published commit_seq, gc_horizon never decreases
- gc_horizon is authoritative ONLY when advanced by the commit sequencer (other processes read-only)

## GC Scheduling Policy (Alien-Artifact)
- f_gc = min(f_max, max(f_min, version_chain_pressure / target_chain_length))
- f_max = 100 Hz (never GC more often than 10ms)
- f_min = 1 Hz (always GC at least once per second)
- version_chain_pressure = observed mean chain length (BOCPD-tracked)
- target_chain_length = 8 (from Theorem 5: R*D+1 for R=100, D=0.07s)
- WHO runs GC: commit coordinator runs raise_gc_horizon() after each group commit batch
- Only the process holding WAL write lock (coordinator) runs GC -- avoids thundering herd
- Other processes observe updated gc_horizon on their next read

## raise_gc_horizon() Algorithm (normative)
- Default: if no active txns, safe point = latest commit_seq
- Scan all TxnSlots:
  - Skip tid==0 (empty)
  - CRITICAL: Sentinel-tagged slots (CLAIMING/CLEANING) are horizon blockers
    - Use min(global_min_begin_seq, old_horizon) for sentinel slots
    - Reason: claiming slot may have captured snapshot but not published real txn_id yet
  - For real TxnId slots: min with slot.begin_seq
- new_horizon = max(old_horizon, global_min_begin_seq) -- monotonic
- Store with Release ordering

## In-Process Version Pruning (§5.6.5.1, REQUIRED)
- Advancing gc_horizon defines reclaimable versions (Theorem 4) but doesn't reclaim memory
- MUST implement incremental, touched-page-driven pruning with strict work budgets
- FORBIDDEN: naive scan-everything-under-VersionArena-write-guard (stop-the-world pauses)

### GcTodo Queue
- GcTodo { queue: VecDeque<PageNumber>, in_queue: HashSet<PageNumber> }
- on_publish_or_materialize_version(pgno): enqueue if not already present
- gc_tick(): pop pages from queue and prune their version chains

### Work Budgets (normative)
- pages_budget = 64
- versions_budget = 4096
- Lock VersionArena.write() only during actual pruning work

### prune_page_chain(pgno, horizon) Algorithm
- Walk chain from head down through versions newer than horizon
- Find committed version <= horizon -> becomes new tail
- Everything older is reclaimable by Theorem 4
- Sever chain: arena[cur].prev_idx = None
- Free all nodes beyond the severed point to free list

### ARC Interaction (normative)
- When committed version removed from chain, its cache entry MUST be eviction-eligible
- Remove (pgno, commit_seq) from ARC indexes and ghost lists (§6.7 coalescing + §6.6 durability)

### I/O Boundary (normative)
- prune_page_chain is pure in-memory work, MUST NOT perform file reads
- If pruned version later needed by old snapshot, resolve() consults durable store (§5.2, §7.11)

PARENT EPIC: bd-3t3
DEPENDS ON: bd-3t3.1 (Core Types), bd-3t3.2 (Invariants/Visibility), bd-3t3.4 (Safety Proofs/Theorem 4-5)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T04:40:25.644030688Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T17:39:21.294486077Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T06:20:12.463097311Z","close_reason":"Content merged into bd-zcdn","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1s71","depends_on_id":"bd-3t3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:09:36.017437493Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1s71","depends_on_id":"bd-3t3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T04:48:08.667735373Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1s71","depends_on_id":"bd-3t3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T04:48:08.774568262Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1s71","depends_on_id":"bd-3t3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T04:48:08.878583950Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":589,"issue_id":"bd-1s71","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA

- [ ] GC horizon correctly computed as min(snapshot.high for all active txns) using safe_gc_seq from shared memory
- [ ] Version pruning never reclaims versions needed by any active or future transaction (Theorem 4)
- [ ] Incremental pruning: prune_page_chain(pgno, horizon) walks chain, frees versions older than horizon
- [ ] Work budget enforced: max 64 pages and 4096 versions per GC tick
- [ ] Pruning is pure in-memory operation (no file I/O during GC)
- [ ] GC scheduling responsive to BOCPD regime shifts (adjusts frequency on workload change)
- [ ] Monotonic coarseness: GC horizon only advances, never retreats
- [ ] Chain tail after pruning: no version older than safe_gc_seq remains
- [ ] Pruning integrates with ARC cache: evicted pages also have chains pruned
","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:52:29Z"}]}

NOTE: The original consolidated spec extract for this rollup lives in the bead comments.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T04:59:02.231938784Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T17:35:14.417456433Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T06:25:14.344189561Z","close_reason":"Content merged into bd-3i98 (P1 §7.4-7.6)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1tnq","depends_on_id":"bd-1nk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:09:36.287271836Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1tnq","depends_on_id":"bd-1qys","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T04:59:30.797594219Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1tnq","depends_on_id":"bd-29vi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T04:59:30.689760288Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":7,"issue_id":"bd-1tnq","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## §7.4 Page-Level Integrity

**On-disk pages:** Standard SQLite has NO per-page checksums. Corruption detected only by structural checks or PRAGMA integrity_check.

**Optional FrankenSQLite enhancement (PRAGMA page_checksum = ON):** Reserved space at end of each page stores XXH3-128 hash:
```
Page layout: [data: page_size - 16 bytes] [xxh3: 16 bytes]
Header byte offset 20 set to 16 (reserved space = 16).
```

C SQLite can read databases with reserved-space checksums (reserved bytes opaque). Default OFF for max interoperability.

**Interoperability Warning:** C SQLite will write zeros/garbage to reserved space when modifying pages, invalidating FrankenSQLite checksum. Database should be Read-Only by legacy clients when page_checksum=ON.

**Verification points:**
- Every disk read: compute XXH3, store in CachedPage
- Every cache read (optional): reverify XXH3
- Before WAL append: verify each page image's integrity hash matches expected
- Before checkpoint write: verify page XXH3

## §7.5 WAL Frame Integrity: Cumulative Checksum Chain

**Append-only integrity:** Inserting or modifying any frame invalidates all subsequent checksums. Detects corruption and tampering.

**Torn write detection:** Partial write produces invalid checksum at torn frame. Recovery reads frames sequentially; first invalid checksum marks valid WAL end.

**Recovery procedure:** Read+verify wal_header checksum (invalid = entirely corrupt, use db file only). Chain from (wal_header.cksum1, wal_header.cksum2). For each frame: verify salts match header (stale frame = stop), verify cumulative checksum. Only committed transactions (last frame has db_size > 0) are replayed.

**Critical implication for self-healing:** Because checksum is cumulative, once mismatch at frame i, WAL format alone cannot validate frames i+1.. (depends on state after frame i). Self-healing MUST provide independent random-access validation. FrankenSQLite: per-source xxh3_128(page_data) in .wal-fec (WalFecGroupMeta.source_page_xxh3_128; S3.4.1) identifies safe source symbols even when chain broken.

## §7.6 Double-Write Prevention

SQLite WAL prevents double-write corruption via:
1. Cumulative checksums (S7.5): torn writes produce invalid checksums
2. Salt values: each WAL generation has unique random salts. After checkpoint RESTART/TRUNCATE, old frames rejected by salt mismatch
3. Commit frame marker: frame with non-zero db_size marks txn boundary. Partial txns (no valid commit frame) discarded during recovery
4. Tightly-packed frames: NOT sector-aligned; 24B header + page_size bytes, no padding. Torn writes detected by cumulative checksum chain, not alignment. (Contrast: rollback journal header IS padded to sector size)

**FrankenSQLite addition:** RaptorQ repair symbols (S3.4.1) turn "detect and discard" into "detect and repair" — corrupted frames within commit group reconstructed if sufficient repair symbols survive.
","created_at":"2026-02-08T04:59:02Z"},{"id":476,"issue_id":"bd-1tnq","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Closed as duplicate of bd-3i98 (§7.4-7.6 Page-Level Integrity + WAL Cumulative Checksum Chain + Double-Write Prevention). Content merged into bd-3i98 comment 110.","created_at":"2026-02-08T07:43:54Z"}]}

NOTE: The original consolidated spec extract for this rollup lives in the bead comments.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T05:17:01.675252424Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T17:32:10.034945674Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T07:54:43.079072429Z","close_reason":"Superseded by bd-3kp.1 (Q1-Q6 with detailed decision register), bd-3kp.2 (R1-R8 with monitoring/triggers), and bd-3bwc (§21.2-21.10 Future Work). No content lost.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1try","depends_on_id":"bd-3kp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:09:36.555882661Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":37,"issue_id":"bd-1try","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## §21 Risk Register + Open Questions + Future Work

### Risk Register (§21.0)
**R1. SSI abort rate too high:** Refine witness keys page→(page,range/cell). Safe snapshot for read-only. Intent-level rebase reduces 30-60%. PostgreSQL ~0.5% at row level; page coarser but merge compensates.
**R2. RaptorQ overhead dominates CPU:** Symbol sizing per object type. Aggressive cache. Profile hot paths (one lever per change).
**R3. Append-only storage grows:** Checkpoint, GC, compaction first-class. Budget enforcement. GC horizon = min(active begin_seq) bounds chain length.
**R4. Bootstrap chicken-and-egg:** Self-describing symbol records. One tiny mutable root pointer. Rebuild-from-scan fallback.
**R5. Multi-process MVCC complexity:** SHM protocol specified (§5.6.1). Lease-based TxnSlot cleanup. Phase 6 validates both in-process and cross-process.
**R6. File format compat vs "do it right":** Compatibility Mode = standard format. Native Mode = innovation. Conformance = observable behavior.
**R7. Mergeable writes correctness minefield:** Strict merge ladder (§5.10.4). Proptest + DPOR. Start small (inserts/updates on leaves), grow guided by benchmarks.
**R8. Distributed mode correctness:** Leader commit clock default. Sheaf checks + TLA+ export. ECS-native replication. Single-node first (Phase 9 for multi-node).

### Open Questions (§21.1)
Q1. Multi-process writer performance envelope → benchmark SHM vs in-process.
Q2. SSI witness key range/cell refinement → start page-only, refine if abort rate unacceptable.
Q3. Symbol sizing per object type → benchmark, expose PRAGMA overrides.
Q4. Compatibility checkpoint without bottleneck → background checkpoint with ECS chunks.
Q5. B-tree operations for deterministic rebase → inserts/updates on leaf first, grow guided.
Q6. B-link style concurrency for hot-page splits → benchmark, add if internal-page conflicts dominate.

### Future Work
§21.2 Cross-Process MVCC: Phase 6 validates, benchmark mmap TxnSlot vs in-process atomics.
§21.3 WAL Multiplexing: Shard by hash(page_number)%num. 2PC across WAL files. For >100K TPS.
§21.4 Distributed Consensus: Raft/Paxos, WAL = replicated log, RaptorQ log shipping, snapshot shipping.
§21.5 GPU-Accelerated RaptorQ: GF(256) maps to SIMD/GPU, 10-50x for K>10K. wgpu.
§21.6 PMEM VFS: Byte-addressable persistent memory, eliminate WAL, 10-100x latency reduction.
§21.7 Vectorized VDBE: Column-at-a-time, SIMD, 2-5x for analytics. Must maintain trigger semantics.
§21.8 Column-Store Hybrid: Per-column B-trees, RLE/dictionary compression, planner selects.
§21.9 Erasure-Coded Page Storage: Group allocation, .db-fec sidecar, checkpoint-only writer, WAL truncation ordering.
§21.10 Time Travel + Tiered Storage: Retention policy, commit_time metadata, SymbolStore pluggable cold backend.
","created_at":"2026-02-08T05:17:01Z"},{"id":64,"issue_id":"bd-1try","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"### Missing Content: §21.3-§21.10 Future Work (Implementation Notes)

**§21.3 WAL Multiplexing (>100K TPS):**
- Shard WAL frames across multiple files: `hash(page_number) % num_wal_files`
- Each WAL file has own checkpoint state
- Commit requires 2PC across WAL files (atomic append to all touched WAL files)
- Crash recovery: replay prepared-but-uncommitted via global commit marker in primary WAL
- Target: NVMe SSDs with high queue depth

**§21.4 Distributed Consensus Integration:**
- Raft/Paxos for replicated state. WAL entries as replicated log.
- Leader handles writes, followers handle reads (read replicas).
- Snapshot shipping (§3.4.3) for new follower init.
- RaptorQ-coded replication (§3.4.2) for steady-state.
- Challenge: linearizable reads (read from leader or read leases).

**§21.5 GPU-Accelerated RaptorQ:**
- GF(256) maps well to SIMD/GPU. Matrix multiplication embarrassingly parallel.
- Expected 10-50x speedup for large source blocks (K > 10,000).
- Framework: wgpu for cross-platform GPU compute.

**§21.6 PMEM VFS:**
- CXL-attached persistent memory: byte-addressable persistent storage.
- Memory-map DB directly to PMEM. Eliminate WAL (copy-on-write + 8-byte atomic pointer swings).
- Use clflush/clwb for cache line persistence.
- MVCC version chains directly in PMEM with epoch-based reclamation.
- Expected 10-100x latency reduction for small transactions.

**§21.7 Vectorized VDBE Execution:**
- Column-at-a-time processing for SIMD utilization.
- Better CPU cache behavior. Expected 2-5x for analytical queries.
- Challenge: maintain row-at-a-time semantics for triggers and RETURNING clause.

**§21.8 Column-Store Hybrid:**
- Column groups in separate B-trees per column.
- Automatic materialization of frequently-scanned columns.
- RLE + dictionary compression for low-cardinality columns.
- Query planner selects row-store or column-store.
- Challenge: consistency under concurrent writes.

**§21.9 Erasure-Coded Page Storage (normative implementation notes):**
- Modified page allocation: allocate G pages as a group.
- Repair storage: ECS (Native) or `.db-fec` sidecar (Compat).
- Read: try source page first, fall back to erasure recovery.
- Benchmark G=32, G=64, G=128 for space/recovery trade-off.
- **Checkpoint-only writer:** In Compat mode, `.db-fec` maintained only by checkpoint (never by txn writers) to avoid group-level write contention.
- **WAL truncation ordering:** RESTART/TRUNCATE checkpoints must not discard WAL unless `.db-fec` updated + fsync'd for affected groups. Degrade to non-truncating checkpoint if behind.

**§21.10 Time Travel Queries + Tiered Symbol Storage (normative implementation notes):**
- Retention policy: time travel meaningful within configured history window. GC/compaction free to drop old history unless retention pins it.
- Addressing: stable coordinate = commit_seq. Timestamp APIs need commit_time metadata + time→commit_seq index (deterministic virtual time under LabRuntime).
- SQL surface: FOR SYSTEM_TIME AS OF (§12.17) + AS OF COMMITSEQ <n>.
- Tiered SymbolStore: pluggable with optional cold backend (object storage). Remote fetch requires RemoteCap (§4.19.1) + caching + prefetching for predictable latency.
","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:14:56Z"},{"id":321,"issue_id":"bd-1try","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Automated Verification (Risk Register Hygiene)

1. **test_risks_have_unique_ids**: R1..R8 unique; Q1..Q6 unique; no renumbering without explicit note.
2. **test_each_high_priority_risk_has_signal**: For each risk with an empirical component (abort rate, overhead, growth), ensure a detection signal exists (benchmark/test/e-process).
3. **test_each_risk_has_mitigation_pointer**: Where applicable, link each risk to at least one mitigation bead (or explicitly mark “unassigned”).

## E2E Review Script (Planned)

- **e2e/risk_register_report.sh**:
  - Emits a summary table of risks/questions.
  - Emits “missing signal” and “missing mitigation” lists.
  - Intended to run periodically to keep the register current.

## Logging Requirements

- Report script emits JSONL lines per risk: `risk_id`, `title`, `signals`, `mitigations`, `status`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Risk register is actionable: every risk has either a mitigation plan or an explicit rationale for deferral.
- The report script can be used to prevent the risk register from becoming stale narrative.
","created_at":"2026-02-08T07:31:02Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1tup","title":"§3 RFC 6330 Conformance Test Suite + HDPC Matrix Verification","description":"## SUMMARY

Verify asupersync's RFC 6330 (RaptorQ) implementation at the mathematical level, covering GF(256) arithmetic tables, systematic index table lookups, LDPC/HDPC constraint generation, and encode/decode roundtrip correctness. Per spec §3.1-3.3: FrankenSQLite's durability, replication, and self-healing all depend on asupersync's production-grade RaptorQ codec. While FrankenSQLite does not reimplement RFC 6330, it MUST verify the codec's correctness at the mathematical building-block level because all storage integrity guarantees flow from it. Key verification targets: GF(256) field with irreducible polynomial 0x11D and generator g=2, the worked example 0xA3 * 0x47 = 0xE1, HDPC matrix construction over GF(256) (not GF(2)), systematic index table (RFC 6330 Table 2, K_max=56403), and decode success probability boundaries (K: ~99%, K+1: ~99.99%, K+2: ~99.99999%).

## KEY DATA STRUCTURES / ALGORITHMS

- **GF(256) field** (§3.2.1): Galois Field with 256 elements, irreducible polynomial p(x) = x^8 + x^4 + x^3 + x^2 + 1 (0x11D), generator g = 2. Addition is XOR. Multiplication via OCT_LOG/OCT_EXP tables (766 bytes base) or MUL_TABLES (64KB, 256x256).
- **OCT_LOG / OCT_EXP tables** (§3.2.1): OCT_LOG[a] = k such that g^k = a (for a != 0). OCT_EXP[k] = g^k. Extended to 510 entries to avoid modular reduction. Multiplication: `OCT_EXP[OCT_LOG[a] + OCT_LOG[b]]`.
- **MUL_TABLES** (§3.2.1): Precomputed 64KB table `MUL_TABLES[a][b] = a * b` in GF(256). O(1) single-lookup multiplication.
- **Systematic index table** (§3.2.5, RFC 6330 Table 2): Maps K to K' (smallest supported K' >= K) with associated parameters J(K'), S(K'), H(K'), W(K'). K_max = 56,403.
- **LDPC constraints** (§3.2.3): S rows, each source column j has exactly 3 nonzeros at positions b, (b+a)%S, (b+2a)%S where a = 1 + floor(j/S), b = j%S. Plus S x S identity block.
- **HDPC constraints** (§3.2.3): H rows of dense GF(256) coefficients from the product MT * GAMMA over GF(256). MT is H x (K'+S), GAMMA is (K'+S) x (K'+S) using alpha (primitive element). Plus H x H identity block. These provide the algebraic strength distinguishing RaptorQ from Raptor (GF(2)).
- **Constraint matrix A** (§3.2.3): L x L matrix where L = K' + S + H. Rows: S LDPC + H HDPC + K' LT constraints. Must be invertible for valid K' values.
- **Inactivation decoder** (§3.3): Two-phase: peeling (remove degree-1 rows) then Gaussian elimination on inactive subset.

## NORMATIVE INVARIANTS

1. **INV-GF256-CORRECT**: OCT_LOG, OCT_EXP, and MUL_TABLES MUST match RFC 6330 §5.7 definitions exactly. Any discrepancy means all RaptorQ operations produce wrong results.
2. **INV-SYSTEMATIC-TABLE**: K' lookups MUST return the correct smallest K' >= K from RFC 6330 Table 2. Wrong K' means wrong matrix dimensions and decode failure.
3. **INV-HDPC-GF256**: HDPC rows MUST use GF(256) coefficients (not GF(2)). At least one HDPC entry must be outside {0, 1}. This is what makes RaptorQ superior to Raptor.
4. **INV-L-FORMULA**: L = K' + S + H MUST hold for all valid K' values. This is the total intermediate symbol count.
5. **INV-DECODE-PROBABILITY**: Decode with exactly K symbols MUST succeed >= 99% of the time. Decode with K+2 symbols MUST succeed >= 99.99999% of the time (§3.1).

## UNIT TEST REQUIREMENTS

- **test_gf256_log_exp_tables_roundtrip**: For every nonzero a (1..=255): assert `OCT_EXP[OCT_LOG[a]] == a`. For every k (0..=254): assert `OCT_LOG[OCT_EXP[k]] == k`. Verify `OCT_EXP[0] == 1` and `OCT_EXP[255] == OCT_EXP[0]`.
- **test_gf256_multiplication_worked_example**: Assert `mul(0xA3, 0x47) == 0xE1`. Verify `OCT_LOG[0xA3] == 91`, `OCT_LOG[0x47] == 253`, `(91+253) % 255 == 89`, `OCT_EXP[89] == 0xE1`. Also: `mul(a, 1) == a` for all a; `mul(a, 0) == 0` for all a; `mul(a, inverse(a)) == 1` for all nonzero a.
- **test_gf256_field_axioms**: For 1000 random triples (a, b, c): verify associativity `mul(mul(a,b),c) == mul(a,mul(b,c))`, distributivity `mul(a, a^b) == mul(a,a) ^ mul(a,b)`, commutativity `mul(a,b) == mul(b,a)`.
- **test_gf256_mul_tables_consistency**: Verify `MUL_TABLES[a][b] == log_exp_multiply(a, b)` for all 65,536 pairs (exhaustive).
- **test_systematic_index_table_lookups**: For K in [1, 5, 10, 50, 100, 1000, 10000, 56403]: look up K' and verify K' >= K and K' is in RFC 6330 Table 2. Verify specific values: K'=6 for K=5, K'=10 for K=10, K'=101 for K=100.
- **test_ldpc_constraint_structure**: For K'=10: construct LDPC rows. Verify each source column j (0..K'-1) has exactly 3 nonzero entries. Verify stride formula. Verify S x S identity block present.
- **test_hdpc_matrix_dimensions_and_gf256_entries**: For K'=10: construct HDPC rows. Verify H rows with (K'+S) GF(256) coefficients from MT*GAMMA. Verify H x H identity block. Verify at least one entry is not in {0, 1} (proving GF(256), not GF(2)).
- **test_encode_decode_roundtrip_multiple_k_values**: For K in [4, 10, 50, 100, 500]: encode K source symbols, decode with exactly K received symbols, verify exact match. Then decode with K+2 symbols and verify > 99.999% success rate across 1000 trials.
- **test_parameter_l_equals_kprime_plus_s_plus_h**: For each K' in the systematic index table: compute S(K'), H(K'), verify L = K' + S + H.

## E2E TEST

- **test_e2e_rfc6330_conformance_vectors**: Run RFC 6330 conformance vectors (or equivalent known-good fixtures) end-to-end through asupersync's encoder and decoder. Verify the tuple generator, systematic index, LDPC/HDPC matrix construction, and decode success at the K, K+1, and K+2 boundaries. On mismatch, log the first divergent intermediate artifact (matrix row, symbol payload, or tuple output) and the random seed for reproduction.

## Logging Requirements

- INFO: vector run summary: `vector_id`, `k`, `overhead`, `result`.
- ERROR: on mismatch, log the first divergent intermediate artifact (matrix row, symbol, or tuple output) and the seed.


## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA

- [ ] GF(256) OCT_LOG/OCT_EXP/MUL_TABLES verified against RFC 6330 §5.7 definitions (exhaustive 65,536-pair check)
- [ ] Worked example 0xA3 * 0x47 = 0xE1 verified with intermediate values (logs 91, 253; sum 89; exp 0xE1)
- [ ] GF(256) field axioms (associativity, distributivity, commutativity, inverse) verified for 1000+ random triples
- [ ] Systematic index table lookups correct for representative K values including K_max=56,403
- [ ] LDPC constraint structure verified: 3 nonzeros per source column with correct stride formula
- [ ] HDPC matrix verified: correct dimensions, GF(256) entries (not GF(2)), identity block present
- [ ] Encode/decode roundtrip succeeds for K in [4, 10, 50, 100, 500] with exact-K and K+2 symbol counts
- [ ] L = K' + S + H identity verified for all K' values in the systematic index table","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:48:05.920856085Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T01:05:43.490548599Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1tup","depends_on_id":"bd-1hi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:49:18.936796735Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1tup","depends_on_id":"bd-1hi.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:38:20.475728147Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":194,"issue_id":"bd-1tup","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# §3 RFC 6330 Conformance Test Suite + HDPC Matrix Verification

## Scope

This bead covers the conformance testing of asupersync's RaptorQ implementation against RFC 6330, focusing on the mathematical building blocks that FrankenSQLite depends on for correctness: the systematic index table, LDPC/HDPC constraint generation, GF(256) arithmetic, and parameter calculation.

While FrankenSQLite uses asupersync's production-grade RFC 6330 implementation (not a re-implementation), we MUST verify its correctness at the mathematical level because FrankenSQLite's durability, replication, and self-healing all depend on it.

## Spec References

- §3.1: K_max = 56,403; T = page_size; failure probability at K is ~1%, at K+1 ~10^-4, at K+2 ~10^-7
- §3.2.1: GF(256) with irreducible polynomial p(x) = x^8 + x^4 + x^3 + x^2 + 1 (0x11D), generator g = 2
- §3.2.1: OCT_LOG/OCT_EXP tables (766 bytes base), MUL_TABLES (64KB), worked example 0xA3 * 0x47 = 0xE1
- §3.2.3: LDPC constraints: 3 nonzeros per source column j with stride a = 1 + floor(j/S)
- §3.2.3: HDPC constraints: MT matrix * GAMMA matrix over GF(256), H rows providing algebraic strength
- §3.2.3: LT constraints via Tuple function, RaptorQ degree distribution (§5.3.5.4 of RFC)
- §3.2.5: Systematic index table (RFC 6330 Table 2): precomputed K' values with J(K'), S(K'), H(K'), W(K')
- §3.3: asupersync modules: gf256.rs, linalg.rs, systematic.rs, decoder.rs, proof.rs, pipeline.rs

## Requirements

### RFC 6330 Test Vector Verification
1. Verify GF(256) arithmetic tables (OCT_LOG, OCT_EXP, MUL_TABLES) match RFC 6330 §5.7 definitions exactly
2. Verify the worked example: 0xA3 * 0x47 = 0xE1 (OCT_LOG[0xA3]=91, OCT_LOG[0x47]=253, (91+253) mod 255 = 89, OCT_EXP[89]=0xE1)
3. Verify GF(256) field properties: a + a = 0 (XOR), a * inverse(a) = 1 for all nonzero a, associativity, distributivity

### Systematic Index Table Lookups
4. For a set of representative K values (1, 5, 10, 50, 100, 1000, 10000, 56403), verify K' lookup returns the correct smallest K' >= K from RFC 6330 Table 2
5. Verify J(K'), S(K'), H(K'), W(K') derivations for each K' value match RFC expectations

### LDPC Constraint Generation
6. For K'=10, construct the LDPC rows and verify: each source column j has exactly 3 nonzeros at positions b, (b+a)%S, (b+2a)%S where a = 1 + floor(j/S), b = j%S
7. Verify the S x S identity block in the LDPC region

### HDPC Matrix Verification
8. For small K' values (6, 10, 20), construct the HDPC rows and verify:
   - MT matrix dimensions: H x (K'+S)
   - GAMMA matrix dimensions: (K'+S) x (K'+S)
   - Product MT * GAMMA yields H rows of GF(256) coefficients
   - H x H identity block in the HDPC region
9. Verify GAMMA matrix structure uses alpha (primitive element of GF(256)) as specified in RFC 6330 §5.3.3.3

### Parameter Calculation
10. Verify L = K' + S + H for multiple K' values
11. Verify that the constraint matrix A is L x L and invertible for valid K' values

## Unit Test Specifications

### Test 1: `test_gf256_log_exp_tables_roundtrip`
For every nonzero element a (1..=255): assert OCT_EXP[OCT_LOG[a]] == a. For every k (0..=254): assert OCT_LOG[OCT_EXP[k]] == k. Verify OCT_LOG[0] is sentinel, OCT_EXP[0] == 1, OCT_EXP[255] == OCT_EXP[0].

### Test 2: `test_gf256_multiplication_worked_example`
Assert mul(0xA3, 0x47) == 0xE1. Also verify: mul(a, 1) == a for all a, mul(a, 0) == 0 for all a, mul(a, inverse(a)) == 1 for all nonzero a.

### Test 3: `test_gf256_field_axioms`
For a random sample of 1000 triples (a, b, c): verify associativity of multiplication (mul(mul(a,b),c) == mul(a,mul(b,c))), distributivity (mul(a, a^b) == mul(a,a) ^ mul(a,b)), commutativity (mul(a,b) == mul(b,a)).

### Test 4: `test_gf256_mul_tables_consistency`
Verify MUL_TABLES[a][b] == log_exp_multiply(a, b) for all 65536 pairs.

### Test 5: `test_systematic_index_table_lookups`
For K in [1, 5, 10, 50, 100, 1000, 10000, 56403]: lookup K' and verify K' >= K and K' is in RFC 6330 Table 2. Verify K'=6 for K=5, K'=10 for K=10, K'=101 for K=100.

### Test 6: `test_ldpc_constraint_structure`
For K'=10: construct LDPC rows. Verify each source column j (0..K'-1) has exactly 3 nonzero entries. Verify the stride formula: a = 1 + floor(j/S), positions at b, (b+a)%S, (b+2a)%S where b = j%S. Verify S x S identity block.

### Test 7: `test_hdpc_matrix_dimensions_and_gf256_entries`
For K'=10: construct HDPC rows. Verify H rows, each with (K'+S) GF(256) coefficients from MT*GAMMA product plus H x H identity. Verify at least one entry is not in {0, 1} (proving GF(256) is used, not GF(2)).

### Test 8: `test_encode_decode_roundtrip_multiple_k_values`
For K in [4, 10, 50, 100, 500]: encode K source symbols, receive exactly K symbols, decode, verify exact match. Then receive K+2 symbols and decode, verify success rate is > 99.999%.

### Test 9: `test_parameter_l_equals_kprime_plus_s_plus_h`
For each K' in the systematic index table: compute S(K'), H(K'), verify L = K' + S + H. Verify L matches the expected total intermediate symbol count.
","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:48:15Z"},{"id":351,"issue_id":"bd-1tup","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## E2E Test

- **test_e2e_rfc6330_conformance_vectors**:
  - Run RFC 6330 conformance vectors (or equivalent known-good fixtures) end-to-end.
  - Verify tuple generator, systematic index, LDPC/HDPC matrices, and decode success boundaries.

## Logging Requirements

- INFO: vector run summary: `vector_id`, `k`, `overhead`, `result`.
- ERROR: on mismatch, log the first divergent intermediate artifact (matrix row, symbol, or tuple output) and the seed.
","created_at":"2026-02-08T07:36:34Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1tup","title":"§3 RFC 6330 Conformance Test Suite + HDPC Matrix Verification","description":"## SUMMARY

Verify asupersync's RFC 6330 (RaptorQ) implementation at the mathematical level, covering GF(256) arithmetic tables, systematic index table lookups, LDPC/HDPC constraint generation, and encode/decode roundtrip correctness. Per spec §3.1-3.3: FrankenSQLite's durability, replication, and self-healing all depend on asupersync's production-grade RaptorQ codec. While FrankenSQLite does not reimplement RFC 6330, it MUST verify the codec's correctness at the mathematical building-block level because all storage integrity guarantees flow from it. Key verification targets: GF(256) field with irreducible polynomial 0x11D and generator g=2, the worked example 0xA3 * 0x47 = 0xE1, HDPC matrix construction over GF(256) (not GF(2)), systematic index table (RFC 6330 Table 2, K_max=56403), and decode success probability boundaries (K: ~99%, K+1: ~99.99%, K+2: ~99.99999%).

## KEY DATA STRUCTURES / ALGORITHMS

- **GF(256) field** (§3.2.1): Galois Field with 256 elements, irreducible polynomial p(x) = x^8 + x^4 + x^3 + x^2 + 1 (0x11D), generator g = 2. Addition is XOR. Multiplication via OCT_LOG/OCT_EXP tables (766 bytes base) or MUL_TABLES (64KB, 256x256).
- **OCT_LOG / OCT_EXP tables** (§3.2.1): OCT_LOG[a] = k such that g^k = a (for a != 0). OCT_EXP[k] = g^k. Extended to 510 entries to avoid modular reduction. Multiplication: `OCT_EXP[OCT_LOG[a] + OCT_LOG[b]]`.
- **MUL_TABLES** (§3.2.1): Precomputed 64KB table `MUL_TABLES[a][b] = a * b` in GF(256). O(1) single-lookup multiplication.
- **Systematic index table** (§3.2.5, RFC 6330 Table 2): Maps K to K' (smallest supported K' >= K) with associated parameters J(K'), S(K'), H(K'), W(K'). K_max = 56,403.
- **LDPC constraints** (§3.2.3): S rows, each source column j has exactly 3 nonzeros at positions b, (b+a)%S, (b+2a)%S where a = 1 + floor(j/S), b = j%S. Plus S x S identity block.
- **HDPC constraints** (§3.2.3): H rows of dense GF(256) coefficients from the product MT * GAMMA over GF(256). MT is H x (K'+S), GAMMA is (K'+S) x (K'+S) using alpha (primitive element). Plus H x H identity block. These provide the algebraic strength distinguishing RaptorQ from Raptor (GF(2)).
- **Constraint matrix A** (§3.2.3): L x L matrix where L = K' + S + H. Rows: S LDPC + H HDPC + K' LT constraints. Must be invertible for valid K' values.
- **Inactivation decoder** (§3.3): Two-phase: peeling (remove degree-1 rows) then Gaussian elimination on inactive subset.

## NORMATIVE INVARIANTS

1. **INV-GF256-CORRECT**: OCT_LOG, OCT_EXP, and MUL_TABLES MUST match RFC 6330 §5.7 definitions exactly. Any discrepancy means all RaptorQ operations produce wrong results.
2. **INV-SYSTEMATIC-TABLE**: K' lookups MUST return the correct smallest K' >= K from RFC 6330 Table 2. Wrong K' means wrong matrix dimensions and decode failure.
3. **INV-HDPC-GF256**: HDPC rows MUST use GF(256) coefficients (not GF(2)). At least one HDPC entry must be outside {0, 1}. This is what makes RaptorQ superior to Raptor.
4. **INV-L-FORMULA**: L = K' + S + H MUST hold for all valid K' values. This is the total intermediate symbol count.
5. **INV-DECODE-PROBABILITY**: Decode with exactly K symbols MUST succeed >= 99% of the time. Decode with K+2 symbols MUST succeed >= 99.99999% of the time (§3.1).

## UNIT TEST REQUIREMENTS

- **test_gf256_log_exp_tables_roundtrip**: For every nonzero a (1..=255): assert `OCT_EXP[OCT_LOG[a]] == a`. For every k (0..=254): assert `OCT_LOG[OCT_EXP[k]] == k`. Verify `OCT_EXP[0] == 1` and `OCT_EXP[255] == OCT_EXP[0]`.
- **test_gf256_multiplication_worked_example**: Assert `mul(0xA3, 0x47) == 0xE1`. Verify `OCT_LOG[0xA3] == 91`, `OCT_LOG[0x47] == 253`, `(91+253) % 255 == 89`, `OCT_EXP[89] == 0xE1`. Also: `mul(a, 1) == a` for all a; `mul(a, 0) == 0` for all a; `mul(a, inverse(a)) == 1` for all nonzero a.
- **test_gf256_field_axioms**: For 1000 random triples (a, b, c): verify associativity `mul(mul(a,b),c) == mul(a,mul(b,c))`, distributivity `mul(a, a^b) == mul(a,a) ^ mul(a,b)`, commutativity `mul(a,b) == mul(b,a)`.
- **test_gf256_mul_tables_consistency**: Verify `MUL_TABLES[a][b] == log_exp_multiply(a, b)` for all 65,536 pairs (exhaustive).
- **test_systematic_index_table_lookups**: For K in [1, 5, 10, 50, 100, 1000, 10000, 56403]: look up K' and verify K' >= K and K' is in RFC 6330 Table 2. Verify specific values: K'=6 for K=5, K'=10 for K=10, K'=101 for K=100.
- **test_ldpc_constraint_structure**: For K'=10: construct LDPC rows. Verify each source column j (0..K'-1) has exactly 3 nonzero entries. Verify stride formula. Verify S x S identity block present.
- **test_hdpc_matrix_dimensions_and_gf256_entries**: For K'=10: construct HDPC rows. Verify H rows with (K'+S) GF(256) coefficients from MT*GAMMA. Verify H x H identity block. Verify at least one entry is not in {0, 1} (proving GF(256), not GF(2)).
- **test_encode_decode_roundtrip_multiple_k_values**: For K in [4, 10, 50, 100, 500]: encode K source symbols, decode with exactly K received symbols, verify exact match. Then decode with K+2 symbols and verify > 99.999% success rate across 1000 trials.
- **test_parameter_l_equals_kprime_plus_s_plus_h**: For each K' in the systematic index table: compute S(K'), H(K'), verify L = K' + S + H.

## E2E TEST

- **test_e2e_rfc6330_conformance_vectors**: Run RFC 6330 conformance vectors (or equivalent known-good fixtures) end-to-end through asupersync's encoder and decoder. Verify the tuple generator, systematic index, LDPC/HDPC matrix construction, and decode success at the K, K+1, and K+2 boundaries. On mismatch, log the first divergent intermediate artifact (matrix row, symbol payload, or tuple output) and the random seed for reproduction.

## Logging Requirements

- INFO: vector run summary: `vector_id`, `k`, `overhead`, `result`.
- ERROR: on mismatch, log the first divergent intermediate artifact (matrix row, symbol, or tuple output) and the seed.


## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA

- [ ] GF(256) OCT_LOG/OCT_EXP/MUL_TABLES verified against RFC 6330 §5.7 definitions (exhaustive 65,536-pair check)
- [ ] Worked example 0xA3 * 0x47 = 0xE1 verified with intermediate values (logs 91, 253; sum 89; exp 0xE1)
- [ ] GF(256) field axioms (associativity, distributivity, commutativity, inverse) verified for 1000+ random triples
- [ ] Systematic index table lookups correct for representative K values including K_max=56,403
- [ ] LDPC constraint structure verified: 3 nonzeros per source column with correct stride formula
- [ ] HDPC matrix verified: correct dimensions, GF(256) entries (not GF(2)), identity block present
- [ ] Encode/decode roundtrip succeeds for K in [4, 10, 50, 100, 500] with exact-K and K+2 symbol counts
- [ ] L = K' + S + H identity verified for all K' values in the systematic index table","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:48:05.920856085Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T02:48:30.075936033Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1tup","depends_on_id":"bd-1hi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:49:18.936796735Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1tup","depends_on_id":"bd-1hi.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:38:20.475728147Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":194,"issue_id":"bd-1tup","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# §3 RFC 6330 Conformance Test Suite + HDPC Matrix Verification

   - MT matrix dimensions: H x (K'+S)

   - Product MT * GAMMA yields H rows of GF(256) coefficients
   - H x H identity block in the HDPC region

## Unit Test Specifications

 {"id":"bd-1uzb","title":"E2E: File Format Compatibility Test Suite (C SQLite Round-Trip)","description":"Cross-cutting E2E test suite verifying FrankenSQLite can read/write standard .sqlite files that C sqlite3 can also read/write.

COVERS: §1.1 (Behavioral Parity) + §11 (File Format) + §17.7 (Conformance Testing)

## TEST SCENARIOS

### Scenario 1: Read C SQLite Files
- test_e2e_read_csqlite_created_db: Open a .sqlite file created by C sqlite3, read all tables, verify data
- test_e2e_read_csqlite_with_indexes: Read DB with multiple indexes, verify index-assisted queries work
- test_e2e_read_csqlite_with_triggers: Read DB with triggers, verify trigger metadata preserved
- test_e2e_read_csqlite_all_page_sizes: Test page sizes 512, 1024, 2048, 4096, 8192, 16384, 32768, 65536
- test_e2e_read_csqlite_with_wal: Open DB in WAL mode created by C sqlite3, verify WAL recovery works

### Scenario 2: Write Files Readable by C SQLite
- test_e2e_write_db_readable_by_csqlite: Create DB with FrankenSQLite, open with C sqlite3, verify data
- test_e2e_write_wal_recoverable_by_csqlite: Write WAL frames, verify C sqlite3 can recover them
- test_e2e_write_header_bytes_match: Compare database header (100 bytes) byte-for-byte with C sqlite3 output

### Scenario 3: Format Edge Cases
- test_e2e_varint_encoding_matches_csqlite: All varint encoding edge cases (1-byte through 9-byte) match exactly
- test_e2e_overflow_pages_match_csqlite: Large BLOBs/TEXT values use overflow pages identically
- test_e2e_freelist_pages_match_csqlite: After DELETE, freelist structure matches C sqlite3
- test_e2e_autovacuum_pointer_map: Auto-vacuum pointer map pages match C sqlite3 layout
- test_e2e_record_format_all_serial_types: All 13 serial type codes encode/decode correctly

### Scenario 4: Encoding
- test_e2e_utf8_text_roundtrip: UTF-8 text (including multibyte) survives round-trip
- test_e2e_utf16le_utf16be_databases: Read/write UTF-16LE and UTF-16BE encoded databases

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
- Log every header field read/written with expected vs actual values
- Log page reads with page number, type, cell count
- Log varint encoding/decoding: value, byte length, raw bytes
- Log any format divergence from C sqlite3 with exact byte offset

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Any .sqlite file created by C sqlite3 3.52.0 can be read by FrankenSQLite
- [ ] Any .sqlite file created by FrankenSQLite can be read by C sqlite3 3.52.0
- [ ] WAL files are cross-compatible between C sqlite3 and FrankenSQLite
- [ ] Database header (100 bytes) is byte-identical for equivalent databases
- [ ] All page sizes from 512 to 65536 supported correctly","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:42:16.478701152Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T01:05:33.657764931Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1uzb","depends_on_id":"bd-1a32","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:42:54.451215254Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1uzb","depends_on_id":"bd-1fpm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T11:03:27.824890725Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1uzb","depends_on_id":"bd-21c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:42:54.828704695Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1uzb","depends_on_id":"bd-lldk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:42:54.635815752Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":640,"issue_id":"bd-1uzb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Unit Test Requirements (Normalization)

This bead is an E2E suite, but we still require unit tests for the harness utilities it relies on:

- test_fixture_roundtrip_builder_deterministic (same seed => identical DB/WAL bytes)
- test_oracle_runner_captures_extended_codes (capture primary + extended result codes, stdout/stderr)
- test_result_diff_is_stable (stable ordering; no nondeterministic diffs)
- test_artifact_bundle_layout (deterministic paths + metadata; CI can consume)

## Logging Requirements (Normalization)

- INFO per case: case_id, seed, page_size, encoding, schema_hash, steps_executed, elapsed_ms
- On failure: persist a minimal repro bundle (db, wal, sql, expected/actual, compact diff summary)
","created_at":"2026-02-08T10:11:15Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1wwc","title":"§8.1-8.2 Workspace Structure + Dependency Layers + Layering Rationale","description":"Covers §8.1 Workspace Structure and §8.2 Dependency Layers for the FrankenSQLite crate architecture. §8.1 defines the 23-crate workspace under crates/, plus supporting directories (conformance/, tests/, benches/, fuzz/, legacy_sqlite_code/). The full crate list: fsqlite-types, fsqlite-error, fsqlite-vfs, fsqlite-pager, fsqlite-wal, fsqlite-mvcc, fsqlite-btree, fsqlite-ast, fsqlite-parser, fsqlite-planner, fsqlite-vdbe, fsqlite-func, fsqlite-ext-{fts3,fts5,rtree,json,session,icu,misc}, fsqlite-core, fsqlite, fsqlite-cli, fsqlite-harness. §8.2 defines a strict 10-layer dependency hierarchy: Layer 0 (leaves: types, error) through Layer 9 (apps: cli, harness). Key normative rules: (1) No crate may depend on a strictly higher layer except where explicitly allowed. (2) fsqlite-mvcc is at L3 (not L6) because B-tree (L4) needs MvccPager trait for page access — the trait definition lives in fsqlite-pager (L2), fsqlite-mvcc (L3) implements it, fsqlite-btree (L4) depends only on the trait, fsqlite-core (L7) wires the concrete impl. (3) fsqlite-wal does NOT depend on fsqlite-pager (breaks compile-time cycle); instead fsqlite-pager defines CheckpointPageWriter trait, and during checkpoint fsqlite-wal receives &dyn CheckpointPageWriter from fsqlite-core. Unit tests required: test_layering_document_matches_cargo_metadata (parse documented layering and verify consistency with cargo metadata edges), test_no_cross_layer_backedges (assert no forbidden reverse-layer dependencies), test_workspace_crate_count_is_23 (assert exactly 23 members under crates/). An e2e/workspace_sanity.sh script should print members and layer assignments, failing on missing members or layering violations. Acceptance criteria: workspace structure and layering rules are mechanically checkable and enforced; checks are cheap enough to run pre-commit.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T05:02:37.528149351Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T19:30:47.578553445Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T19:30:47.578524992Z","close_reason":"Workspace validation: 7 tests (crate count, layer assignments, cross-layer backedges, wal-pager cycle break, mvcc layer, supporting dirs, e2e). Removed stale wal→pager dep. cargo metadata integration. All 23 crates validated.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1wwc","depends_on_id":"bd-3an","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:09:36.818432304Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":12,"issue_id":"bd-1wwc","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## §8.1 Workspace Structure

23 crates under `crates/`, plus supporting directories:
- crates/: fsqlite-types, fsqlite-error, fsqlite-vfs, fsqlite-pager, fsqlite-wal, fsqlite-mvcc, fsqlite-btree, fsqlite-ast, fsqlite-parser, fsqlite-planner, fsqlite-vdbe, fsqlite-func, fsqlite-ext-{fts3,fts5,rtree,json,session,icu,misc}, fsqlite-core, fsqlite, fsqlite-cli, fsqlite-harness
- conformance/: Golden output fixtures
- tests/: Workspace integration tests
- benches/: Criterion benchmarks
- fuzz/: Fuzz targets (excluded from workspace)
- legacy_sqlite_code/: C source reference

## §8.2 Dependency Layers (10 layers)

```
Layer 0 (leaves):     fsqlite-types    fsqlite-error
Layer 1 (storage):    fsqlite-vfs      fsqlite-ast
Layer 2 (cache):      fsqlite-pager    fsqlite-parser     fsqlite-func
Layer 3 (log+mvcc):   fsqlite-wal      fsqlite-mvcc       fsqlite-planner
Layer 4 (btree):      fsqlite-btree
Layer 5 (vm):         fsqlite-vdbe
Layer 6 (ext):        fsqlite-ext-{fts3,fts5,rtree,json,session,icu,misc}
Layer 7 (core):       fsqlite-core
Layer 8 (api):        fsqlite
Layer 9 (apps):       fsqlite-cli      fsqlite-harness
```

**Layering rationale (V1.7 errata):**
- fsqlite-mvcc moved from L6 to L3: B-tree (L4) needs MvccPager trait for page access. MvccPager trait definition lives in fsqlite-pager (L2); fsqlite-mvcc (L3) implements it. fsqlite-btree (L4) depends only on fsqlite-pager (L2) for the trait; fsqlite-core (L7) wires the concrete impl.
- fsqlite-wal does NOT depend on fsqlite-pager (breaks cycle): fsqlite-pager defines CheckpointPageWriter trait. During checkpoint, fsqlite-wal receives &dyn CheckpointPageWriter from fsqlite-core. Both depend on fsqlite-vfs and fsqlite-types without cycles.
","created_at":"2026-02-08T05:02:37Z"},{"id":315,"issue_id":"bd-1wwc","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Unit Tests / Automated Checks Required

1. **test_layering_document_matches_cargo_metadata**: Parse the documented layering (Layer 0..9) and verify it is consistent with `cargo metadata` edges.
2. **test_no_cross_layer_backedges**: Assert no crate depends on a strictly higher layer except where explicitly allowed by the spec (e.g., harness/cli depending on api/core).
3. **test_workspace_crate_count_is_23**: Assert the workspace has exactly 23 members under `crates/` as specified.

## E2E Test Script

- **e2e/workspace_sanity.sh** (planned):
  - prints the workspace members and layer assignment
  - fails if any member is missing, renamed, or violates layering

## Logging Requirements

- Scripts must output a stable, grep-friendly summary:
  - `member_count`, `members_missing`, `layer_violations_count`
  - list violations as one-per-line records: `from -> to`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- The workspace structure and layering rules are mechanically checkable and enforced.
- The checks are cheap enough to run before every commit.
","created_at":"2026-02-08T07:29:46Z"},{"id":634,"issue_id":"bd-1wwc","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] 23 crates under crates/ directory matching spec §8.1 workspace structure
- [ ] 10-layer dependency hierarchy (L0 leaves through L9 apps) enforced
- [ ] No cross-layer backedges: no crate depends on strictly higher layer
- [ ] fsqlite-mvcc at L3 (not L6): trait definition in fsqlite-pager (L2), implementation in fsqlite-mvcc (L3)
- [ ] fsqlite-wal does NOT depend on fsqlite-pager (cycle broken via CheckpointPageWriter trait)
- [ ] cargo metadata validates: dependency graph matches documented layers
- [ ] Supporting directories present: conformance/, tests/, benches/, fuzz/, legacy_sqlite_code/
- [ ] Layering check script runs pre-commit and catches violations
","created_at":"2026-02-08T10:01:32Z"},{"id":671,"issue_id":"bd-1wwc","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## E2E Test (Named)

- test_e2e_bd_1wwc: implement the E2E plan already described in this bead; follow bd-1fpm for repro-bundle logging.","created_at":"2026-02-08T17:59:57Z"}]}
